content.verdict: Villa Mitirapa is a private four-bedroom house on Matira Point, right on the beach — the same beach the St. Regis paid a fortune to sit near. For honeymooners this is an unusual pick but a smart one under specific conditions: you are traveling with another couple or family, you want total privacy rather than resort service, or you are celebrating a milestone (five-year anniversary, post-honeymoon babymoon) where having a full house is part of the luxury. The villa sleeps up to 8, has a private pool, direct beach frontage, a full kitchen, optional chef and housekeeping, and an elevated living room that looks straight out over the lagoon. For two people the villa is obviously overkill — but the nightly rate divided across two couples is typically lower per-person than overwater bungalows at the top resorts, and you get infinitely more space, a private beach stretch, and nobody else on property. No butler culture, no kids-club noise, no dining queues. Bring groceries from Vaitape or hire the chef; the villa makes cooking in part of the romance. For couples who think resort life feels performative, this is the antithesis.
content.best_room: The master bedroom upstairs is the honeymoon pick — ocean-facing, largest bathroom with soaking tub, direct access to the upper deck. If traveling with friends, ensure you are in the master and they are in the secondary bedrooms on the other side of the house for privacy. Book the full villa, not a room-only rate (the whole point is exclusive use).
caveats: This is a rental villa, not a hotel — expect vacation-home operations: self check-in, a housekeeping schedule rather than daily turndown, and the need to organize your own food.
Chef, housekeeping and babysitting are add-ons, not included.
For two people alone the economics are questionable unless you want maximum privacy.
No resort pool, no spa on-site, no room service culture.
Requires planning — groceries, restaurant bookings, transport.
Not the place if you want to be taken care of end-to-end.

faqs.q: Does it make sense for just two people?
faqs.a: Only if privacy is your non-negotiable. For two couples or a small family it is outstanding value. For two people alone, a one-bedroom beach villa at Eden Beach or a boutique overwater at Pearl Beach is usually better economics.

faqs.q: Is there daily housekeeping and a chef?
faqs.a: Housekeeping is typically included every other day (confirm with the owner). A private chef can be booked à la carte — highly recommended for a honeymoon dinner night. Daily maid service can be added for a surcharge.
The villa is beachfront on Matira — steps from sand. Matira Beach is one of the best beaches in French Polynesia.

faqs.q: Do we need a rental car?
faqs.a: Recommended. Taxis on Bora Bora are expensive and infrequent; a rental car (about $100/day) gives you freedom to explore the island.

faqs.q: Can we get overwater bungalow experience nearby?
faqs.a: Many couples do a split stay: 3-4 nights at Villa Mitirapa, 3-4 nights overwater at Hilton or Le Meridien. This combines privacy with the iconic overwater shot.

The villa is marketed through vacation rental agencies and direct owner channels. Book through a Virtuoso-affiliated agent if possible — better insurance, pre-arrival concierge, and price-match guarantees.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon / anniversary stay — Villa Mitirapa, [dates]

Hi,

We are planning a honeymoon on Bora Bora and would like to enquire about booking Villa Mitirapa for 7 nights arriving [date].

Please confirm:
- Availability and full-villa exclusive-use rate for our dates
- What housekeeping cadence is included
- Private chef pricing (we would like one dinner and possibly a breakfast service)
- Grocery pre-stocking option
- Rental car and transfer recommendations
- Spa treatment access at a nearby resort (we will pay directly)
- Any other villas or experiences you would recommend for a honeymoon

Thank you,
[Names]
